name: Classes should not have too many methods
description: A class with too many methods is difficult to understand and maintain. Consider splitting responsibilities.

name: Methods should not be too complex
description: The cyclomatic complexity of methods should not exceed a threshold. High complexity indicates hidden boundaries or mixed concerns.

name: Public fields should not be used
description: Public fields violate encapsulation. Use accessors instead to maintain boundary contracts.

name: Cognitive Complexity of functions should not be too high
description: High cognitive complexity indicates functions that do too much. Consider splitting into smaller, well-defined functions.

name: A callback function should not throw exceptions
description: Unhandled exceptions in callbacks can indicate missing error boundary or poor isolation.

name: String literals should not be duplicated
description: Duplicated strings may indicate missing constants or enums at contract boundaries.

name: Calling 'setState' directly in render
description: Setting state in render() indicates boundary violation between presentational and state management logic.

name: Functions should not be empty
description: Empty functions may indicate incomplete implementation or missing abstraction.

name: Cognitive Complexity of functions should not be too high
description: High cognitive complexity indicates functions that do too much. Refactor into smaller, focused functions.

name: Function names should comply with a naming convention
description: Inconsistent naming may indicate mixed concerns or missing module-level organization.

name: Cognitive Complexity of functions should not be too high
description: Functions with high cognitive complexity often violate single responsibility principle.

name: Depending on component implementation details
description: Tests or code depending on component internals indicate tight coupling. Use public interfaces instead.

name: String literals should not be duplicated
description: Duplicated strings may indicate missing constants or enums at contract boundaries.

name: Cyclic module dependencies should be avoided
description: Circular dependencies create tight coupling and hidden boundaries. Refactor to eliminate cycles.

name: Catch exceptions instead of the generic Exception
description: Catching generic exceptions indicates missing error boundary definition.

name: Large files should be split
description: Large files often contain multiple responsibilities. Refactor into smaller, focused modules.

name: Classes should not be too complex
description: Classes with high complexity often violate separation of concerns. Consider splitting into multiple classes.

name: TODO/FIXME comments indicate incomplete boundary definition
description: TODOs may indicate uncertain architectural boundaries. Clarify or document the decision.
